div.head: [Untitled]

opener.dateline: Extract from the speech of Mr. J. Q. Adams, in the House of Representatives, April 13, 1846.

div.p: * * * “I am not for settling the question at the line of 49°.” * * * “If this House pass this, and instead of putting down ‘south of the line of 49°,’ as is proposed by this amendment, will say ‘south of latitude 54° 40′,’ I will vote for it.” * * * “Great Britain had no claim whatever. I believe she has no pretensions to any now.”
